_meta.keyKnowledge: We are learning that different types of texts across all our subjects are set up differently and use different language features depending on their purpose.

_source.w: We are learning that different types of texts across all our subjects are set up differently and use different language features depending on their purpose.
y: Recognising text structures helps us read and write more efficiently in every subject.
Different purposes need different text types — and knowing this makes us flexible communicators.
This understanding helps us create the right kind of text for any situation.
eg: We compare how a science report is structured versus a narrative writing piece.
We identify the features of a procedure text — title, materials, steps — in a recipe.
We notice that an information text uses headings while a story uses paragraphs.
We look at texts from different subjects and identify their purpose and features.
We match text types to their purposes — to inform, to persuade, to entertain, to instruct.
We plan our own writing by first deciding on our purpose and then choosing the right structure.
